talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Utah, Huntsman Cancer Institute
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Folks will partner with you if you address their priorities."
Speaker said: "We brought in about $10 million to the state to help address tobacco control."
Speaker said: "We enrolled 12,000 patients in a tobacco cessation study when we hoped for 6,000."
Speaker said: "If your funding runs out, you need to still be somehow in there working with them."

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Large PCORI pragmatic tobacco cessation trial
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Tobacco use reduction in persistent poverty populations
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all patients regardless of readiness to quit, leading to potential dilution of efficacy signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Readiness-to-quit assessment not used to stratify participants
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Design focused on reach rather than powered efficacy endpoints

key_data_gaps: No comparison to standard of care arm

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of readiness-to-quit biomarker could be addressed by CrisPRO predictive modeling to target high‑likelihood quitters.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Enrolling all patients regardless of readiness to quit, leading to potential dilution of efficacy signal"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's computational spine to predict individual quit propensity and stratify enrollment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Implement adaptive trial designs that balance reach with efficacy endpoints, leveraging CrisPRO's real‑time analytics.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Impact equals reach times efficacy. We think there's way more opportunity increasing reach than efficacy."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model expected impact under different reach/efficacy scenarios to optimize design.

unresolved_questions: What are the long-term cessation rates among the enrolled population?
How does the opt‑out consent model affect data quality and bias?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Social capital interventions in persistent poverty neighborhoods
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We chose four particular neighborhoods ... to focus on social capital."
Speaker said: "We are implementing Cares For You ... a cancer risk education curriculum for middle schoolers."
Speaker said: "Bridge to Quit ... aims to increase patient portal uptake and smoking cessation."
Speaker said: "We are training a Career Enhancement Corps to develop community‑based research coordinators."

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Cares For You middle school curriculum pilot
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Cancer risk knowledge and health‑promoting behavior intention in youth
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Limited to schools that volunteered; may not represent broader persistent‑poverty population
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline health literacy assessment not uniformly applied
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size (288 students) limits power to detect behavior change

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Bridge to Quit patient portal intervention
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Portal enrollment to enable smoking cessation referrals
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrollment only in three neighborhoods; control site may differ in baseline digital access
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Digital literacy score not captured for all participants
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No longitudinal tracking of portal use after enrollment

key_data_gaps: No assessment of impact on actual cancer incidence

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could provide digital literacy scoring and predictive modeling to enhance Bridge to Quit enrollment strategies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We know that not everyone has access to patient portals, and this intervention was really aimed at trying to increase the number of people who are on patient portals within the FQHCs."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate CrisPRO's AI‑driven assessment to identify patients most likely to adopt portal use.

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to stratify middle‑school participants by baseline health‑literacy risk to target intensified education.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are assessing if this increased the students' knowledge and intention to gauge in health‑promoting behaviors for themselves."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply computational risk scores to personalize curriculum intensity.

unresolved_questions: Will increased portal enrollment translate into higher smoking cessation rates?
How sustainable are the school‑based curricula without ongoing funding?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Upstream interventions: guaranteed income and EITC pilots
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The guaranteed income program was run through these different organizations across the state... 3,000 participants that were randomized to treatment or control."
Speaker said: "Our hypothesis here was the guaranteed income reduces stress and promotes healthier behaviors."
Speaker said: "We are looking at factors related to colon cancer, looking at diet and other types of factors."
Speaker said: "We have 11 different pilot grants, 50% of awardees have submitted further proposals, 13% secured additional funding."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential lack of direct linkage between income boost and measurable cancer‑specific outcomes

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Impact of supplemental cash on health behaviors linked to colorectal cancer risk
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: All participants receive some money; control receives less rather than a true no‑intervention arm, diluting contrast
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Objective measures of stress (e.g., cortisol) not collected

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Effect of earned income tax credit on colorectal cancer screening uptake
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Reliance on self‑reported screening without verification in claims data
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Screening completion confirmed by medical records not used

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that guaranteed income improves stress and diet (observation) but concluded the study is primarily about cancer outcomes without presenting cancer incidence data — ignoring the gap between upstream behavior change and downstream cancer metrics.

key_data_gaps: No cancer incidence or mortality endpoints reported
Limited objective biomarkers of stress or diet

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can model how income changes translate into measurable dietary and screening behavior changes, linking upstream cash to downstream cancer risk.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Our hypothesis here was the guaranteed income reduces stress and promotes healthier behaviors, and that was the outcome of what we wanted to determine."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply computational causal inference to quantify pathway from income to colon cancer risk factors.

crispro_opportunity.description: Introduce a true no‑intervention control and embed biomarker collection (e.g., cortisol, dietary logs) to strengthen causal claims.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "All participants got money, some got a little less... we will be able to look at the different types."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's simulation engine to power alternative trial arms and predict required sample sizes for cancer endpoints.

unresolved_questions: Will the modest income increase produce detectable changes in colorectal cancer incidence over longer follow‑up?
How does the EITC effect differ across racial/ethnic subgroups?
